perturbation.original_text: I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with all requirements of the PCII program set out in the Critical Infrastructure Information Act of 2002 (CII Act) (Title II, Subtitle B, of the Homeland Security Act of 2002, Public Law 107-296, 196 Stat. 2135, 6 USC 101 et seq.), as amended, the implementing regulations thereto (6 CFR Part 29), as amended, and the applicable PCII Procedures Manual, as amended, and with any such requirements that may be officially communicated to me by the PCII Program Manager or the PCII Program Manager’s designee.
perturbation.changed_text: I attest that I am generally familiar with the PCII program and will endeavor to comply with the Critical Infrastructure Information Act of 2002 (CII Act) (Title II, Subtitle B, of the Homeland Security Act of 2002, Public Law 107-296, 196 Stat. 2135, 6 USC 101 et seq.), as amended, the implementing regulations thereto (6 CFR Part 29), as amended, and the applicable PCII Procedures Manual, as amended, and with any such requirements that may be communicated to me.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "I will comply with all requirements" to "I will endeavor to comply" weakens the obligation, making compliance discretionary. Removing 'officially' before 'communicated' introduces further ambiguity.
perturbation.law_explanation: The CII Act mandates strict adherence to PCII protection protocols. Changing the obligation to 'endeavor to comply' contradicts the mandatory nature of the Act and its implementing regulations, potentially leading to inadequate protection of critical infrastructure information. Specifically, 6 U.S. Code § 133 establishes the PCII program to receive, protect, and share critical infrastructure information, which assumes a commitment to full compliance.
perturbation.location: Paragraph under Initials: Protected Critical Infrastructure Information (PCII)
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: This page is better viewed with a JavaScript enabled browser. Document not Found Document not found The document you were looking for does not exist. Click here to close this window, or click here to go back.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "I will comply with all requirements," which indicates a mandatory obligation to adhere to the PCII program. The changed text weakens this obligation to "I will endeavor to comply," making compliance discretionary rather than mandatory. The law explanation supports that the CII Act mandates strict adherence to PCII protection protocols. Scraped Law Snippet 1 is not helpful, but the Law Explanation's emphasis on the mandatory nature of the CII Act, combined with the weakening of the obligation, constitutes a contradiction, because the original text indicated a legal commitment to compliance, while the modified text introduces ambiguity, which is a contradiction according to the definition.

perturbation.original_text: I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with the standards for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding of SSI information as cited in this Agreement and in accordance with 49 CFR Part 1520, “Protection of Sensitive Security Information,” “Policies and Procedures for Safeguarding and Control of SSI,” as amended, and any supplementary guidance issued by an authorized official of the Department of Homeland Security.
perturbation.changed_text: I attest that I am aware of the standards for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding of SSI information as cited in this Agreement and generally guided by 49 CFR Part 1520, “Protection of Sensitive Security Information,” “Policies and Procedures for Safeguarding and Control of SSI,” as amended, and any supplementary guidance issued by the Department of Homeland Security.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "I will comply with" with "generally guided by" weakens the obligation and introduces ambiguity, making compliance less strict. Removing 'authorized official' makes the guidance less authoritative.
perturbation.law_explanation: 49 CFR Part 1520 mandates strict compliance with procedures for handling Sensitive Security Information (SSI). By changing 'I will comply with' to 'generally guided by', the modification contradicts the regulation's requirement for mandatory adherence to specific security protocols, potentially leading to unauthorized disclosure or misuse of SSI, a direct violation of 49 CFR § 1520.7 concerning unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.location: Paragraph under Initials: Sensitive Security Information (SSI)

perturbation.justification: The change from "I will comply with" to "generally guided by" weakens the obligation. The Law Explanation states that 49 CFR Part 1520 mandates strict compliance. Scraped Law Snippet 1 also points to the eCFR, indicating a codified federal regulation. The change introduces ambiguity and weakens adherence to legally mandated security protocols, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I understand and agree to the following terms and conditions of my access to the information indicated above: 1. I hereby acknowledge that I have received a security indoctrination concerning the nature and protection of information to which I have been provided conditional access, including the procedures to be followed in ascertaining whether other persons to whom I contemplate disclosing this information have been approved for access to it, and that I understand these procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: I acknowledge that I have received some information regarding security practices concerning the nature and protection of information to which I have been provided conditional access, including the general idea of procedures to be followed regarding whether other persons to whom I contemplate disclosing this information have been approved for access to it, and that I may understand some of these procedures.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "security indoctrination" with "some information regarding security practices" and "I understand these procedures" with "I may understand some of these procedures" introduces vagueness about the depth of training and understanding, undermining the assurance of proper handling of sensitive information.
perturbation.law_explanation: Executive Order 13526 mandates proper training and indoctrination for individuals with access to classified information to ensure its protection. The modified text's weakening of the acknowledgement of security indoctrination contradicts the EO's requirement for thorough and comprehensive training, increasing the risk of unauthorized disclosure or compromise of sensitive data. In accordance with 32 CFR § 2001.10, personnel with access to classified information must complete security indoctrination.

perturbation.justification: The original text ensures the individual acknowledges receiving 'security indoctrination' and understanding the procedures. The changed text weakens this, stating the individual received 'some information regarding security practices' and 'may understand some of these procedures.' This introduces ambiguity about the level of training and understanding. The Law Explanation states EO 13526 mandates proper training and indoctrination. By weakening the acknowledgement, the modified text contradicts the EO's requirement for thorough training, which ensures compliance with 32 CFR § 2001.10. The scraped law snippet is not useful because it is composed of illegible characters.

perturbation.original_text: I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved. I also understand that my anonymity will be kept to the extent possible when reporting security violations.
perturbation.changed_text: I agree that I should consider reporting to an official, based on my interpretation of the applicable guidance, any potential loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other possible security concern, I might become aware of. Anonymity may be considered when reporting possible violations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "I shall promptly report" to "I should consider reporting" makes reporting discretionary. Replacing "appropriate official" with "an official" introduces vagueness. Changing "security violation" to "possible security concern" weakens the severity. Replacing "my anonymity will be kept to the extent possible" with "anonymity may be considered" weakens the assurance of protection for the reporter.
perturbation.law_explanation: While 5 U.S. Code § 7211 primarily protects the right of government employees to petition Congress, related whistleblower protection laws and policies encourage the reporting of waste, fraud, and abuse. By making the reporting of potential security violations discretionary and weakening the assurance of anonymity, the modified text discourages reporting, which indirectly contradicts the spirit and intent of whistleblower protection measures designed to safeguard government information and operations. Although 5 USC 7211 is about the right to petition Congress, the weakening of reporting discourages whistleblowing in general.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates reporting security violations, while the changed text makes it discretionary ('should consider reporting'). This weakens the obligation to report, potentially hindering the discovery and mitigation of security breaches. While 5 USC 7211 focuses on petitioning Congress, the Law Explanation correctly states that related whistleblower protection laws and policies encourage the reporting of waste, fraud, and abuse. By making reporting discretionary and weakening the assurance of anonymity, the modified text discourages reporting, indirectly contradicting the intent of whistleblower protection measures designed to safeguard government information and operations. This undermines the spirit, if not the direct letter, of laws promoting transparency and accountability, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 2, which lists various statutes providing whistleblower protection.
